project.name: The Dark Candle
project.purpose: A data-driven voxel game built on Bevy with real-world SI physics, procedural planetary generation, and multi-resolution octree storage. Targets both Linux (Wayland) and Windows.

key_dependencies: bevy 0.18 — ECS game engine (Wayland + X11 on Linux)
serde 1.0 — serialization for all data structs
ron 0.8 — Rusty Object Notation for data files
noise 0.9 — procedural noise (Perlin) for terrain generation
wgpu 27.0 — headless GPU compute (atmosphere renderer, particle simulation)

entry_points: src/main.rs — CLI parsing, plugin registration, Bevy app startup
tests/simulations.rs — headless simulation test harness entry point
src/bin/worldgen.rs — standalone CLI binary: planetary generation pipeline + stats output

major_modules.name: world
major_modules.responsibility: Chunks, octree SVO, terrain generation (flat + spherical) with composable NoiseStack (FBM/ridged/selector/warp/micro-detail), continent/ocean masks, biome-terrain integration (slope/altitude-aware surface materials), depth-based geological strata & ore veins, multi-scale caves (caverns/tunnels/tubes), hydraulic erosion (droplet + grid), meshing (Surface Nets), D8 flow erosion, LOD, raycast, voxel access layer, planet config, refinement/subdivision

major_modules.name: physics
major_modules.responsibility: Five physics solvers: LBM gas (D3Q19 wind/pressure/moisture), AMR fluid (Navier-Stokes rivers), FLIP/PIC (particle rain/snow/debris), gravity/buoyancy/drag forces, collision detection. Plus atmosphere model, structural integrity, SI constants

major_modules.name: chemistry
major_modules.responsibility: Heat transfer (Fourier conduction + Stefan-Boltzmann radiation), chemical reactions (RON-defined neighbor-pair matching), state transitions (melt/boil/freeze with latent heat). Fire propagation is emergent from these systems, not a separate module

major_modules.name: lighting
major_modules.responsibility: Orbital sun mechanics, Rayleigh+Mie atmospheric scattering (CPU for export, Bevy Atmosphere component for in-game GPU sky), sunlight propagation/light maps, volumetric cloud ray-marching, terrain shadows, cloud shadows, distance fog with time-of-day color, day/night cycle

major_modules.name: weather
major_modules.responsibility: Cloud-to-particle precipitation emission, GPU particle readback/rendering, LBM-to-GPU wind field upload, ground accumulation (snow/rain/sand deposits)

major_modules.name: gpu
major_modules.responsibility: Headless wgpu compute: atmosphere renderer (1000× speedup over CPU), particle physics compute (100k particles), terrain noise eval (93× speedup), GPU context initialization with try_new() fallback. WGSL shaders in src/gpu/shaders/

major_modules.name: data
major_modules.responsibility: RON asset loading pipeline: MaterialRegistry (sync at startup), plus lazy Bevy asset loading for enemies, creatures, items, props, trees, fluid config. Dual-indexed material lookup by ID and name

major_modules.name: simulation
major_modules.responsibility: Deterministic headless test harness: RON-defined scenarios with composable geometry regions, multi-tick loop (reactions → heat → transitions → pressure), assertion types for physics verification

major_modules.name: procgen
major_modules.responsibility: Procedural generation: recursive tree branching into octree, biome selection/weighted spawning, creature generation, item templates, slope-aware prop decoration, chunk spawn planning

major_modules.name: biology
major_modules.responsibility: Lifecycle (Juvenile→Adult→Elder), Kleiber's law metabolism, health with 6 damage types, corpse decomposition, vegetation spreading. Data structures complete but plugin systems not yet wired

major_modules.name: behavior
major_modules.responsibility: Utility-based AI: needs (hunger/thirst/fatigue/fear), A* pathfinding, line-of-sight/sound/smell perception, behavior execution (idle/wander/chase/eat/attack/sleep). Data structures complete but plugin systems not yet wired

major_modules.name: social
major_modules.responsibility: Factions with territory/disposition, flocking/formations, creature relationships, reputation tracking. FactionRegistry initialized but systems not yet wired

major_modules.name: persistence
major_modules.responsibility: Save/load with 3 manual slots + autosave. Persists player state, RLE-compressed chunks, creatures, items, enemies, factions. SaveId system for stable entity references. Version 3 with migration

major_modules.name: diagnostics
major_modules.responsibility: F3 frame budget overlay (EMA FPS/frame time), F11 ECS state dump (RON), F12 screenshot, per-system timing, video capture (ffmpeg/PNG fallback), software raymarching visualization

major_modules.name: camera
major_modules.responsibility: First-person camera with gravity toggle (G key). Walk 5 m/s, sprint 8 m/s, fly 20 m/s. Tangent-plane movement for spherical terrain. Spherical mode: camera orientation aligned to local surface normal via Quat::from_rotation_arc base rotation. 1.7m eye height, realistic jump physics

major_modules.name: hud
major_modules.responsibility: Health bar (gradient), temperature display (K→°C), 24-hour clock with day/night icons, 8-point compass, fall damage system (7 m/s threshold)

major_modules.name: interaction
major_modules.responsibility: DDA raycast block targeting (6-voxel reach), block break/place, 9-slot hotbar (scroll/number keys), crosshair, block outline highlight

major_modules.name: game_state
major_modules.responsibility: State machine: Loading → Playing ↔ Paused. Loading screen, pause menu with save/load slot UI, virtual time control

major_modules.name: audio
major_modules.responsibility: Material-based footstep sounds (stone/dirt/wood/sand/water), block break/place sounds, looping wind ambient, UI click

major_modules.name: entities
major_modules.responsibility: Basic enemy component and data-driven spawning framework. Minimal — serves as template for entity expansion

major_modules.name: planet
major_modules.responsibility: Standalone planetary generation pipeline: icosahedral geodesic grid, tectonic simulation, astronomical impact events, celestial mechanics (star/moons/rings/Keplerian orbits), static climate model, biome classification, geological rock/ore assignment. Used by the worldgen binary and planned for future game integration.

major_modules.name: map
major_modules.responsibility: In-game map overlay: local discovery map (biome-colored chunk columns, fog-of-war, zoom) and global planet map (equirectangular projection with player lat/lon marker, zoom/pan). Discovery tracking persisted in save files.

patterns: Data-Driven ECS: All game data loaded from .ron files via bevy_common_assets; no hardcoded stats
SI Physics: All physical properties use real-world SI units; emergent behavior only, no magic numbers
1 voxel = 1 meter spatial mapping throughout the engine
Sparse Voxel Octree (SVO): Generic OctreeNode<T> with Morton indexing for multiresolution storage
MaterialRegistry: Synchronous loading at startup with dual-indexed lookup (by ID u16 and by name String)
Async chunk management: AsyncComputeTaskPool for terrain generation and mesh building
Plugin architecture: Each module registers as a Bevy plugin with ordered system sets
Simulation test harness: Declarative RON scenarios with composable geometry and assertion framework
GPU compute via raw wgpu: Atmosphere and particle pipelines bypass Bevy's render graph
RLE compression for chunk persistence; SaveId system for stable cross-session entity references
Surface Nets meshing algorithm (smooth meshes without lookup tables)
Force-based physics: F_net = gravity + buoyancy + drag + friction; acceleration = F_net / mass
notes: LOD mesh generation: generate_mesh_generic() takes scale:f32 parameter. Non-LOD passes 1.0, LOD passes lod_step as f32. Vertex positions multiplied by scale to span full chunk extent.

conventions.naming: RFC 430: snake_case functions/variables, PascalCase types. Named MaterialId constants (never numeric literals). Kebab-case for .ron file names
conventions.file_structure: One Bevy plugin per module directory with mod.rs. Data structs derive serde::Deserialize + bevy::asset::Asset + TypePath. All .ron data files in assets/data/ with descriptive suffixes (.material.ron, .reaction.ron, .biome.ron, etc.)
other: Lines under 100 characters; rustfmt enforced; clippy warnings treated as errors (-D warnings)
No unwrap()/expect() in library code — use Result with ? operator
Prefer &T over cloning; use &str for function parameters; iterators over index-based loops
All physics use real-world SI units — no arbitrary constants or magic numbers
Eagerly implement Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, From, AsRef on types
/// rustdoc comments for all public APIs with examples
Unit tests in #[cfg(test)] mod tests alongside code
Pre-commit hook: cargo fmt --check → cargo clippy -D warnings → cargo test --lib
Bevy 0.18: NO bundles (deprecated), spawn components as tuples, set_if_neq() for state transitions

IcosahedralGrid::all_areas() — dual-cell areas via Van Oosterom–Strackee, sum = 4π
phases.notes: 15 unit tests. Y-up convention: pos.y = sin(latitude). Level 7 = 163,842 cells. 12 pentagon cells at all levels.

phases.name: Tectonic simulation
implements: run_tectonics(data, progress_cb) — entry point, step count derived from TectonicMode × tectonic_age_gyr
run_tectonics_with_history(data, progress_cb, max_frames) — variant that captures TectonicHistory snapshots for time-lapse
TectonicMode enum — Quick (dt=60 Myr), Normal (dt=15 Myr), Extended (dt=5 Myr)
fibonacci_sphere_points() — uniform seed placement for plate origins
weighted_bfs_flood_fill() — Pareto power-law growth weights (α=1.3, max ratio 15×)
init_plates() — physical angular velocities (2–10 cm/yr surface speed, SI rad/yr)
evolve_plate_velocities() — acceleration model with bounce at speed bounds, perturbation every ~50 Myr
detect_boundaries(dt_myr) — velocity dot-product classifies Convergent/Divergent/Transform
apply_boundary_forces(dt_myr) — dt-scaled height changes per boundary type
subduction_advance(dt_myr) — dt-scaled reassignment, plates below 1% surface protected
erode(dt_myr) — dt-scaled neighbor diffusion
compute_sea_level() — area-weighted percentile targeting 30% land
phases.notes: 26 unit tests. Power-law plate sizes (few large oceanic, many small continental). Geological time calibrated to real SI: velocities in rad/yr, rates in per-Myr, all dt-scaled via REF_DT_MYR=10. Three configurable modes (Quick ~50 steps, Normal ~200, Extended ~600 at default 3.0 Gyr). TectonicHistory captures ≤100 snapshots (~2.5 MB/frame at level 7) for time-lapse visualization.

phases.name: Astronomical impact events
implements: run_impacts(data) — applies giant impact + stamps all ordinary craters
generate_impact_events(intensity, seed) — log-uniform size distribution
stamp_crater(data, event) — BFS footprint + radial depth profile (bowl/rim/ejecta)
apply_giant_impact(data, seed) — hemisphere depression + antipodal uplift
crater_depth_profile(r) — piecewise: bowl r<0.5, rim r<1.0, ejecta r<2.0
phases.notes: 13 unit tests. BFS depth-limited to MAX_BFS_DEPTH=8 hops. Ejecta covers 2× crater radius.

phases.name: Celestial mechanics
implements: CelestialSystem::generate(mass, radius, seed) — star + moons + ring + orbital params
solve_kepler(m, e) — Newton-Raphson, ε<1e-12 in ≤10 steps
moon_position(moon, t) — 3-1-3 Euler rotation (Ω, i, ω) into inertial frame
CelestialSystem::tidal_height_at(cell_pos, t) — P₂(cosθ) equilibrium formula
CelestialSystem::find_eclipses(start, end, dt) — angular radius solar/lunar detection
CelestialSystem::star_direction_at(t) — planet orbital position at time t
phases.notes: 14 unit tests. Star: log-uniform mass [0.6–1.8]×M☉, L∝M^4, T∝M^0.505. Moons: 0–4, Kepler 3rd law periods. Rings: ~15% probability.

phases.name: Climate and biome assignment
implements: run_biomes(data) — populates temperature_k, precipitation_mm, ocean_proximity, biome
equatorial_temperature(data) — Stefan-Boltzmann energy balance: (L★(1−α)/(16πd²σ))^0.25 + 33K greenhouse
cell_temperatures(data, t_equator) — lat gradient (60K×sin²lat) + lapse rate (6.5K/km)
ocean_proximity_bfs(data) — O(n) BFS from ocean cells, 20-hop linear decay
cell_precipitation(data, temp, prox) — Clausius-Clapeyron humidity × orographic reduction
assign_biomes() — 14-type simplified Whittaker diagram classification
phases.notes: 20 unit tests. 14 biomes: Ocean, DeepOcean, IceCap, Tundra, BorealForest, ColdSteppe, TemperateForest, Alpine, TropicalSavanna, TropicalRainforest, HotDesert, ColdDesert, Wetland, Mangrove. Earth-like system yields 287.6K (14.4°C) global mean. Must run before run_geology.

phases.name: Geological layering and ore placement
implements: run_geology(data) — populates surface_rock, geological_age, ore_deposits
assign_surface_rocks(data) — 10 RockTypes from tectonic context
compute_geological_age(data) — boundary-type base + volcanic boost + deterministic noise
place_ore_deposits(data, rocks) — per-cell SmallRng with 7 ORE_* bitmask constants
phases.notes: 8 unit tests. 10 rock types: Basalt, Granite, Sandstone, Limestone, Shale, Marble, Quartzite, Obsidian, Peridotite, Gneiss. 7 ore types: ORE_IRON/COPPER/GOLD/COAL/SULFUR/GEMS/OIL. Must run after run_biomes.

phases.name: Worldgen CLI binary
implements: main() — sequential pipeline: new PlanetData → tectonics → impacts → biomes → geology
--stats flag: full output of grid, tectonic, impact, celestial, biome, geology stats
--level, --seed, --radius-km CLI arguments
--tectonic-mode <quick|normal|extended>: sets TectonicMode (default: normal)
--tectonic-age <Gyr>: simulated geological age in billions of years (default: 3.0)
--timelapse flag: when combined with --globe, captures TectonicHistory and enables playback mode
phases.notes: Full pipeline at level 4 (2562 cells) runs in ~11 ms. Level 7 (163842 cells) ~400 ms. Run with: cargo run --bin worldgen -- --seed 42 --level 4 --stats. Tectonic modes: quick (~50 steps, 0.2s), normal (~200 steps, 0.8s), extended (~600 steps, 2.4s) at level 7. Time-lapse: --globe --timelapse captures tectonic evolution snapshots.

phases.name: Interactive globe renderer

run_globe_viewer(data) — Bevy app with orbital camera, 8 colour modes, star/moon/ring overlays

switch_colour_mode — keys 1-8, +/- exaggeration, rebuilds mesh on change
screenshot_system — F12 saves PNG via Bevy screenshot API
compute_smooth_normals() — face-normal accumulation for terrain-aware lighting (replaces radial normals)

playback_controls system — Space (play/pause), Left/Right (step), Home/End (jump), Period/Comma (speed)
advance_playback system — Timer-driven frame advance, auto-stops at end
apply_snapshot system — overlays snapshot data onto PlanetData, triggers mesh rebuild
phases.notes: 7 unit tests. Colour modes: elevation, biome, plates, geological age, crust depth, tidal amplitude, rock, temperature. CLI: --globe flag. Globe mesh now has noise displacement + geometry-derived normals via detail.rs. Time-lapse playback: --timelapse captures snapshots (≤70 frames, ~2.5 MB/frame at level 7) with interactive controls (Space: play/pause, arrows: step, >/<: speed).

phases.name: Map projection export
implements: Projection enum: Equirectangular, Mollweide, Orthographic

CLI: --projection, --output, --width, --colourmode, --animate flags
Two-pass detailed rendering: Pass 1 computes interpolated+noise elevation grid, Pass 2 applies hillshading
phases.notes: 10 unit tests. CPU rasterization, no GPU needed. Equirect 512px in ~80ms at level 4. Projections now use terrain detail layer for sub-cell noise + hillshading.

phases.name: Terrain detail layer
implements: TerrainNoise::new(seed) — FBM (6 octaves, freq 50) + RidgedMulti (4 octaves, freq 35) on unit sphere
terrain_roughness(biome, boundary, volcanic, elevation) — per-cell noise amplitude from biome/tectonic context
interpolate_elevation(grid, elevations, lat, lon) — IDW from nearest cell + neighbors using angular distance

hillshade_pixel(HillshadeParams) — NW cartographic illumination (azimuth 315°, altitude 45°) from elevation grid
phases.notes: 10 unit tests. Adds sub-cell noise variation + smooth interpolation between cells + hillshading for relief. Used by both projections.rs (2D maps) and render.rs (3D globe mesh displacement + geometry-derived normals).

phases.name: GPU-accelerated terrain projection
implements: GpuProjectionRenderer — uploads cell data, spatial index, neighbor adjacency to GPU storage buffers
terrain_projection.wgsl: elevation_pass — inverse projection + spatial index nearest-cell + IDW + hash-based 3D Perlin FBM/ridged noise
terrain_projection.wgsl: hillshade_pass — 3×3 stencil gradient + NW illumination + elevation/cell colour + shade modulation
render_projection_gpu() — convenience wrapper matching CPU API, dispatches two 16×16 compute passes
render_animation_gpu() — GPU-accelerated rotating orthographic animation
phases.notes: Hash-based Perlin noise (no permutation table) produces visually similar but not bit-identical output to CPU path. 35× speedup at 4096px. CLI: --gpu flag.

phases.name: Planetary terrain connection

--planet-level (default 5) and --planet-seed (default 42) CLI args

phases.notes: BiomeType→MaterialId and RockType→MaterialId mappings in planetary_sampler.rs. Ore vein placement from ore_bitmask at 3% sparsity. Cave carving reuses SphericalTerrainGenerator noise. Thread-safe via Arc<> cloning into AsyncComputeTaskPool. RON asset loading now active: planet_config.ron is loaded via AssetServer and applied to overwrite default PlanetConfig.

phases.notes: Three cave layers: caverns (freq 0.01), tunnels (freq 0.04), tubes (freq 0.025). Underground lakes and lava tubes. 12 geology tests.
